name: Pickleball Chiro — Instagram Weekly Content Audit

parameters.jsCode:
// Split gradedPosts array back into individual items for Google Sheets
const { gradedPosts, weekOf } = $json;

return (gradedPosts || []).map(p => ({ json: {
  'Post ID': p.id,
  'Week Of': weekOf,
  'Post Date': p.postDate,
  'Day Posted': p.dayOfWeek,
  'Type': p.mediaType,
  'Pillar': p.pillar,
  'Hook': p.hook,
  'CTA Keyword': p.ctaKeyword || '',
  'Reach': p.reach,
  'Impressions': p.impressions,
  'Views': p.videoViews || '',
  'Completion %': p.completionRate != null ? p.completionRate : '',
  'Saves': p.saves,
  'Shares': p.shares,
  'Comments': p.comments,
  'Likes': p.likes,
  'Profile Visits': p.profileVisits,
  'Follows': p.follows,
  'Performance Score': p.performanceScore,
  'Grade Label': p.gradeLabel,
}}));

// Build the Claude API request body
const { promptData, weekOf } = $('Grade and Summarize').first().json;

const systemPrompt = `You are the content strategist for @dr.lane_o, the Pickleball Chiro brand.

Lane is a 5.0 DUPR competitive pickleball player and licensed chiropractor targeting serious recreational players aged 45-65 who fear being forced off the court by injury. His audience is "Dave" — a 52-year-old who plays 4x per week, has dealt with knee or shoulder issues, and is terrified of slowing down.

Four content pillars:
1. Pain & Injury — speaks to Dave's fear of injury ending his game
2. Performance & Technique — validates Dave's competitive identity
3. Recovery & Longevity — positions Lane as the answer to staying on court long-term
4. Credibility & Story — builds trust through Lane's dual identity (5.0 player + DC)

Active ManyChat keywords: KNEE, FREE

Metric priorities (in order): reach (non-follower), completion rate, shares, saves, profile visits. Likes are a vanity metric — deprioritize.

Core philosophy: The goal of every post is to OPEN A LOOP — mirror, validate, tease. Never fully explain. Hooks are everything. A bad hook on great content kills the post. Lane sounds like a competitive peer who happens to be a DC — not a clinical expert lecturing.`;

const userMessage = `Here is the Instagram performance data for @dr.lane_o for the week of ${weekOf}:

${promptData}

Write the weekly Sunday content brief with these sections:

## WHAT WORKED THIS WEEK (AND WHY)
Analyze each DOUBLE DOWN or MODIFY post. What hook patterns drove performance? What does this tell us about what Dave is thinking about right now?

## WHAT FAILED AND THE DIAGNOSED REASON
For each RETIRE or KILL IT post: was it the hook, the pillar, the format, or the CTA? Be specific.

## CONTENT TO DOUBLE DOWN ON THIS WEEK
Specific angles, hook styles, and pillars to push more of this week.

## CONTENT TO RETIRE
Specific angles to stop using based on this week's data.

## 3 HOOK IDEAS FOR NEXT WEEK
Write three ready-to-use hooks in Lane's voice — casual, competitive-peer tone. Include pillar and format (Reel/Carousel) for each.

## PILLAR RECOMMENDATION FOR THE WEEK
Which pillar gets the most posts this week and why?

## EXPERIMENT OF THE WEEK
One specific new angle or hook style to trial as a test. Include what success looks like.`;

return [{ json: {
  model: 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514',
  max_tokens: 2000,
  system: systemPrompt,
  messages: [{ role: 'user', content: userMessage }],
  _weekOf: weekOf,
}}];
